title: VOIDRUNNER #1 variant-cover pack — one inked hero illustration spun into six print-ready graphic-novel variant covers (vectorized line-art, Ben-Day/duotone/grain/glitch FX treatments, a licensed grunge-distress incentive variant, spot-colour separations) plus an Illustrator data-merged trade-dress run that stamps issue #/colourway/price/barcode-zone per variant from a CSV, all rendered print-ready out of Illustrator

evidence: image 1: armored runner leaping neon night-city rooftop — matches brief subject exactly
trade_dress_csv.render: credit_line reads 'Cover by Artist A - Colours by Colorist B' not 'Cover by Elias Thorne - Colours by Zara Vance' per cover_spec

image 1: Colour fills present, but not pure ink line-art as required
image 2: Logo is not clearly vectorized, only raster PNG supplied

fix.problem: Logo resolution is lower than requested (~2500px).
fix.prompt_delta: Ensure the logo lockup is approximately 2500px on its longest side.

No separate per-variant raster files (coverA_standard.png … coverF_sketch.png) present — though these are workflow outputs, the CSV references them as linked-file Variable inputs the merge template expects
All required data and image assets are present.
All mandatory data fields in JSON and CSVs are populated.

image 2: No vector SVG of logo, only raster

fix.problem: Source image 1024x1536px is ~72dpi equivalent at comic trim; print requires ~2000x3075px minimum, brief says ~4500px
fix.prompt_delta: Regenerate hero cover illustration at minimum 2000x3075px (target 4500px tall), full-bleed comic trim proportions 6.625x10.25in at 300dpi, armored runner in clear mid-leap pose clearing rooftop edge.

evidence: trade_dress_merge.csv headers (cover_art, issue_no, colourway_name, variant_ratio, cover_price, credit_line, variant_code, barcode_zone) match recorded Variable names exactly — document_merge_data_vector can consume directly
image 1 has busy neon-city background suitable for image_select_subject and image_apply_halftone; image 2 has transparent ground (checkerboard visible) suitable for image_vectorize

trade_dress_csv.render: CSV headers match Variables, ready for data-merge
spot_sep_csv.render: Spot separation CSV matches template spec
deductions: image 1 at 1024px true dims risks pixelation artifacts when image_crop_and_resize targets 300dpi comic trim — may force agent to upscale
logo_lockup (image 2) is landscape 1536x1024px but a wordmark needs to be raster-imperfect for image_vectorize; current render looks already clean/vector-like, reducing vectorize value-add
Hero cover art resolution (1024x1536) is too low for 300dpi print-ready output (1988x3075px).
Logo resolution (1536x1024) is lower than requested, impacting vectorization quality.
image 1: Source is 1024x1536px, below 4500px/print spec; may limit print quality
image 2: Logo is raster, not SVG, so not directly reusable for vector placement

fix.problem: Hero cover art resolution is too low for print-ready 300dpi output.
fix.prompt_delta: Regenerate the hero cover art at a minimum resolution of 1988x3075 pixels to meet print-ready 300dpi requirements for a 6.625x10.25in comic trim+bleed.

evidence: image 1 reads as AI-generated illustration — flat mid-tones, no genuine ink line-art separation, cape anatomy slightly off; brief says 'approved inked hero cover'
image 2 logo is clean digital type with a light grunge filter — passes as a distressed masthead but lacks the hand-crafted roughness of a real indie comic logo

Cover spec JSON contains realistic and comprehensive comic production metadata.
image 2: Logo PNG is plausible, but vector would be more typical for pro workflow

fix.problem: Image reads as digital painting not inked line-art with flat colour fills; fails 'approved inked hero cover' realism bar
fix.prompt_delta: Redraw as comic book ink line-art style: bold black ink outlines, flat colour fills (no gradients), cel-shaded neon city background, visible ink hatching on shadows, consistent with professional comic book interior art.

Palette elements (cyan, magenta) are consistent with the brand's defined colours.

evidence: Adobe Stock grunge texture correctly demoted to live execution decision — not synthesized, matches brief's 'licensed' requirement
Illustrator .ai templates correctly recorded as user-authored, not generated — sound rationale for document_merge_data_vector binding
DECISIONS.REQ: User-authored Illustrator trade-dress template
trade_dress_csv.render
Decisions: All user-authored template and stock assumptions are industry-standard
No real input wrongly demoted; all gaps justified
deductions: Decision records cover_art Variable as linking to coverA_standard.png…coverF_sketch.png but these per-variant processed rasters are workflow outputs not yet generated — the merge will fail at runtime if they are absent
credit_line placeholder values in CSV were not caught as a data-quality risk in decisions
Assumptions about user-authored Illustrator templates and stock sourcing are sound and justified.
The 'credit_line' in trade_dress_csv.render uses generic names, contradicting the authoritative cover_spec.json.

fix.problem: 'credit_line' in CSV uses generic names, not specific names from cover_spec.json.
fix.prompt_delta: Update 'credit_line' column in CSV to use specific names from `cover_spec.json`: 'Cover by Elias Thorne - Colours by Zara Vance'.

top_risks: CLIENT-REJECT (sev 9): The hero cover art (hero_cover_art) is only 1024x1536px — nowhere near the ~4500px specified and fatally under-resolution for a 300dpi comic trim+bleed print file; it will pixelate on press and cannot be salvaged by the workflow.

AGENT CHOKE on 'hero_cover_art': image_vectorize and every downstream raster step require a high-res source; at 1024x1536px the agent will either fail the resolution gate or produce a blown-out, artifact-ridden vectorization that makes all six variant covers unprintable.
